{"step_by_step_thinking": "Dimorphic fungi are fungi that can exist in two different forms: a yeast form and a mold form. Pneumocystis jirovecii is known to be an atypical fungus that does not exhibit dimorphism. It primarily exists as a cyst form and does not change into a mold form. On the other hand, Histoplasma capsulatum, Blastomyces dermatitidis, and P. marneffi can exist in both yeast and mold forms, making them dimorphic fungi.", "answer_choice": "D"}
